articles.title: Toward a Modular Architecture for Embedded AI Agent Systems at the Edge
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02862v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rise of Large Language Models (LLMs) has enabled agentic AI capable of complex reasoning and tool use; however, deploying such autonomy in pervasive computing environments remains challenging due to the strict memory and energy constraints of embedded microcontrollers. Existing frameworks typically assume server-class resources or continuous connectivity, leaving a gap for deeply embedded systems. This paper proposes a modular reference archit
articles.title_zh: Toward a Modular Architecture for Embedded AI Agent Systems at the Edge

articles.title: Evaluating Transformer and LSTM Frameworks for Prediction in Ungauged Basins
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02791v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Watershed networks exhibit convergent topologies in which multiple tributaries merge into downstream channels,integrating diverse upstream hydrological processes. In ungauged basins, the absence of direct observations increases uncertainty and limits the ability to anticipate extreme events. This study evaluates whether an encoder-only Transformer provides an advantage over an LSTM for upstream streamflow inference under limited hydrologic informa
articles.title_zh: Evaluating Transformer and LSTM Frameworks for Prediction in Ungauged Basins

articles.title: Don't Gamble, GAMBLe: An Analytical Framework for AI-Driven Research Systems
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02863v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI-Driven Research Systems (ADRS) -- systems coupling LLMs with automated evaluation to discover algorithms, proofs, and designs -- are being optimized and adopted across domains, but the tools to analyze them have not kept pace. ADRS performance depends on component interactions that are poorly understood, expensive to explore, and (as we show) not well captured by standard convergence guarantees. These guarantees rely on structural assumptions t
articles.title_zh: Don't Gamble, GAMBLe: An Analytical Framework for AI-Driven Research Systems

articles.title: Large AI Models in Dental Healthcare: From General-Purpose Systems to Domain-Specific Foundation Models
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02914v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Background: Oral diseases affect nearly 3.5 billion people worldwide, yet the comparative clinical potential of large-scale AI models in dentistry remains poorly understood. Three distinct model categories have emerged: language-generative models, discriminative vision foundation models, and dental-specific foundation models, with no unified review examining their relationships and collective limitations.
  Methods: Following PRISMA-ScR guidelines
articles.title_zh: Large AI Models in Dental Healthcare: From General-Purpose Systems to Domain-Specific Foundation Models

articles.title: Traj-Evolve: A Self-Evolving Multi-Agent System for Patient Trajectory Modeling in Lung Cancer Early Detection
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02812v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Modeling patient trajectories from longitudinal electronic health records (EHRs) requires reasoning over sparse, noisy, and long-context multimodal sequences. Existing LLM-based multi-agent systems address context length but process patients in isolation, failing to mirror how clinicians leverage accumulated experience from similar prior cases. We present Traj-Evolve, a self-evolving multi-agent system with two complementary evolving mechanisms. F
articles.title_zh: Traj-Evolve: A Self-Evolving Multi-Agent System for Patient Trajectory Modeling in Lung Cancer Early Detection

articles.title: An Exploration of Collision-based Enemy Morphology Generation
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02832v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Despite a great deal of prior research into Procedural Content Generation (PCG), relatively little prior work has explored generating enemies for video games. In particular, there is almost no work on generating enemy morphologies, the basic body plan or collision information for in-game enemies, despite the existence of related morphology generation work in robotics. In this paper, we explore three different novel approaches to generate enemy mor
articles.title_zh: An Exploration of Collision-based Enemy Morphology Generation

articles.title: WISE-HAR: A Generalizable Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for WiFi-Based Human Activity Recognition
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02974v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using WiFi signals has emerged as a transformative technology for smart homes, healthcare monitoring, security systems, and ambient assisted living. Unlike traditional camera-based systems that raise significant privacy concerns and fail in low-light conditions, or wearable sensors that require user compliance, WiFi-based HAR is non-intrusive, privacy-preserving, cost-effective, and works seamlessly in any lighting
articles.title_zh: WISE-HAR: A Generalizable Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for WiFi-Based Human Activity Recognition

articles.title: ChatHealthAI: Aligning Electronic Health Record Representations with Large Language Models for Grounded Clinical Reasoning
articles.description:
arXiv:2606.02802v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong natural-language reasoning abilities for clinical decision support, but struggle to effectively model structured longitudinal electronic health records (EHRs). In contrast, EHR foundation models can learn predictive patient representations, yet lack interpretable language-based reasoning. To bridge this gap, we propose ChatHealthAI, a multimodal reasoning framework that aligns structured EHR representati
articles.title_zh: ChatHealthAI: Aligning Electronic Health Record Representations with Large Language Models for Grounded Clinical Reasoning
